auctions.title: 1995 Craig Craft 168 Boss “Porsche Boat”
auctions.thesis: This 1995 Craig Craft 168 Boss is a 16′ speedboat that is equipped with a GM-derived 4.3-liter V6 paired with a Berkeley jet drive. The fiberglass hull is finished in teal and features elements intended to resemble a 1990s Porsche including curved bodywork, round headlights, taillights, and side mir...
auctions.history: This 1995 Craig Craft 168 Boss is a 16′ speedboat that is equipped with a GM-derived 4.3-liter V6 paired with a Berkeley jet drive. The fiberglass hull is finished in teal and features elements intended to resemble a 1990s Porsche including curved bodywork, round headlights, taillights, and side mirrors.

auctions.title: 2008 Porsche Cayenne Turbo
auctions.thesis: This 2008 Porsche Cayenne Turbo is finished in Crystal Silver Metallic over Black Full leather and has remained registered in California since new. It is powered by a twin-turbocharged 4.8-liter V8 paired with a six-speed automatic transmission and is equipped with the Dark Wood Package, Porsche Dyn...
auctions.history:
This 2008 Porsche Cayenne Turbo is finished in Crystal Silver Metallic over Black Full leather and has remained registered in California since new. It is powered by a twin-turbocharged 4.8-liter V8 paired with a six-speed automatic transmission and is equipped with the Dark Wood Package, Porsche Dynamic Chassis Control, 21″ Cayenne Sport wheels, a back-up camera, heated front seats, and a Bose sound system. The seller purchased the vehicle in 2025 and has since added approximately 2k of the 165k indicated miles. This Cayenne Turbo is now offered with an accident-free Carfax report and a clean California title in the name of the seller’s spouse.

The vehicle is finished in Crystal Silver Metallic with black trim and features bi-xenon headlights, a receiver hitch, a sunroof, roof rails, parking sensors, and a rear window wiper. Damage is visible on the front bumper cover, right rocker panel, and rear bumper cover, and scratches can be seen around the body.

The 21″ Cayenne Sport alloy wheels have curb marks and are mounted with 295/35 Michelin Latitude Sport tires. The crossover is equipped with Porsche Dynamic Chassis Control (PDCC), an air suspension, and Porsche Active Suspension Management (PASM). Stopping power is from Porsche-branded calipers over ventilated rotors at each corner. The seller reports replacing the inner and outer tie rods at 165k miles.

The heated power-adjustable front seats have debossed Porsche crests on the headrests and are upholstered in Black leather that extends to the dashboard, center console, and door panels. Amenities include a back-up camera, a Bose sound system, dual-zone automatic climate control, and front-seat memory settings. Blower motor repairs are noted in the Carfax report in January 2024. The headliner is sagging and has been pinned.

The wood- and leather-rimmed multifunction steering wheel is equipped with shift buttons and frames factory instrumentation. The digital odometer indicates 165k miles, approximately 2k of which were added under current ownership.

The twin-turbocharged 4.8-liter V8 was factory rated at 500 horsepower and 516 lb-ft of torque. The Carfax report notes camshaft repairs in July 2011, starter replacement in May 2021, and coolant reservoir replacement in May 2024. The seller tells us that the oil was changed, and the lower heater core coolant pipe and ignition coils were replaced at 165k miles.

Power is routed to all four wheels through a six-speed Tiptronic S automatic transmission, a dual-range transfer case, and a variable center differential. Catalytic converter replacement is noted in the Carfax report in June 2022.

The Carfax report is free of accidents or other reported damage and notes at least one open recall. The crossover passed a California emissions test in January 2026.

auctions.title: 29k-Mile 2010 Porsche 911 Carrera 4S Coupe 6-Speed
auctions.thesis: This 2010 Porsche 911 Carrera 4S is a black-over-black coupe that was optioned with a six-speed manual transaxle, and it was purchased new by the owner at Barrier Porsche in Bellevue, Washington. Powered by a 3.8-liter flat-six, the car has 29k miles and is further equipped with an all-wheel-drive s...
auctions.history:
This 2010 Porsche 911 Carrera 4S is a black-over-black coupe that was optioned with a six-speed manual transaxle, and it was purchased new by the owner at Barrier Porsche in Bellevue, Washington. Powered by a 3.8-liter flat-six, the car has 29k miles and is further equipped with an all-wheel-drive system, bi-xenon headlights, Dynamic Cornering Lights, a sunroof, 19″ Sport Design wheels, PCM with navigation, heated and ventilated power-adjustable front seats, a Bose sound system, and automatic climate control. This 997.2 C4S coupe is now offered by the seller on behalf of the owner in California with a window sticker, a clean Carfax report, and a Washington title.

The 997 Carrera 4S features wider rear bodywork and a reflective strip between the taillights when compared to the standard Carrera. This example is finished in black and has bi-xenon headlights, Dynamic Cornering Lights, integrated fog lights, a sunroof, a speed-activated rear spoiler, auto-dimming mirrors, and quad exhaust outlets.

The 19″ Sport Design wheels are mounted with Michelin tires showing 2016 and 2017 date codes. Braking is handled by red-finished Porsche-branded calipers over cross-drilled rotors, and the car is equipped with Porsche Active Suspension Management (PASM).

The heated and ventilated power-adjustable front seats are upholstered in black leather, which extends to the dashboard and door panels. Porsche-branded floor mats line the footwells, and additional appointments include PCM with navigation, a Bose sound system, automatic climate control, and cruise control.

The leather-wrapped steering wheel frames a central tachometer and a 200-mph speedometer as well as gauges for oil temperature, oil pressure, coolant temperature, and fuel level. The digital odometer indicates 29k miles, all of which were added under current ownership.

The 3.8-liter flat-six features VarioCam Plus variable valve timing for a factory rating of 385 horsepower and 310 lb-ft of torque. An oil change was performed in 2025.

Power is routed to all four wheels via a six-speed manual transaxle.

The window sticker lists initial delivery to Barrier Porsche in Bellevue, Washington, along with factory colors, equipment, options, and a total price of $109,835.

The Carfax report is free of accidents or other reported damage.
The car has not passed a California emissions inspection.

auctions.title: 1998 Porsche 911 Carrera S Coupe 6-Speed
auctions.thesis: This 1998 Porsche 911 Carrera S coupe is finished in Ocean Blue Metallic over Cashmere Beige partial leather and is powered by a 3.6-liter flat-six linked with a six-speed manual transaxle and an optional limited-slip differential. Equipment includes a Fister exhaust system, a sunroof, a speed-activ...
auctions.history:
This 1998 Porsche 911 Carrera S coupe is finished in Ocean Blue Metallic over Cashmere Beige partial leather and is powered by a 3.6-liter flat-six linked with a six-speed manual transaxle and an optional limited-slip differential. Equipment includes a Fister exhaust system, a sunroof, a speed-activated rear spoiler, and 18” wheels along with a power-adjustable driver’s seat, cruise control, air conditioning, and a Porsche Classic stereo. The car was sold new in New York and remained registered in the state until 2020, and it was acquired by the seller out of Massachusetts in mid-2021. This 993 C2S is offered with a window sticker, a Porsche Certificate of Authenticity, owner’s manual, service records, a clean Carfax report, and a clean California title in the seller’s name.

The 993 C2S featured wider bodywork, a split rear grille, and revised bumper covers and side skirts in comparison with the contemporary Carrera. This example is finished in Ocean Blue Metallic (L3AY, L3AZ) and has headlight washers, fog lights, power-adjustable mirrors, a power-operated sunroof, a speed-activated rear spoiler, a rear window wiper, and dual exhaust outlets. Records indicate that paint protection film was fitted to the front bumper, hood, fenders, and side mirrors before a ceramic coating was applied in 2021. Photos of paint meter readings are provided in the gallery.

The 18″ wheels are mounted with 225/40 front and 285/30 rear Pirelli P Zero Rosso tires. Braking is handled by Porsche-branded calipers over cross-drilled rotors at all four corners. The front and rear sway bar bushings and end links were replaced in 2020.

The seats are trimmed in Cashmere Beige partial leather, and cabin features include a power-adjustable driver’s seat, cruise control, and air conditioning. A Porsche Classic stereo and replacement Porsche speakers have been installed. The air conditioning system was repaired in 2021, and the shifter was retrimmed in 2022. The seller notes wear in the interior.

The leather-wrapped steering wheel frames VDO instrumentation including a central tachometer with a 6,800-rpm redline, a 180-mph speedometer, an analog clock, and gauges for oil pressure, oil temperature, oil level, and fuel level. The six-digit odometer shows 26k miles, approximately 5,500 of which were added under current ownership. The speedometer was repaired in 2021.

The air-cooled 3.6L M64 flat-six features a VarioRam intake system and was factory rated at 282 horsepower and 250 lb-ft of torque. A Fister Stage 2 exhaust system was installed in 2020, at which time the drive belts were replaced. The fan belt, alternator belt, spark plugs, and valve cover gaskets were replaced in 2024. An oil change was performed in January 2026. The car passed a California emissions inspection in July 2025.

Power is sent to the rear wheels via a six-speed manual transaxle and an optional limited-slip differential.

The Porsche Certificate of Authenticity notes factory colors, equipment, and a production completion date of October 24, 1997.

The Carfax report is free of accidents or other reported damage.
Filed under: 993

auctions.title: 2026 Porsche 911 GT3 6-Speed
auctions.thesis: This 2026 Porsche 911 GT3 is finished in Oak Green Metallic Neo over black and gray upholstery and is powered by a 4.0-liter flat-six mated to a six-speed manual transaxle and a Porsche Torque Vectoring Plus electronically locking differential. Equipment includes a swan-neck rear wing, Porsche Activ...
auctions.history:
This 2026 Porsche 911 GT3 is finished in Oak Green Metallic Neo over black and gray upholstery and is powered by a 4.0-liter flat-six mated to a six-speed manual transaxle and a Porsche Torque Vectoring Plus electronically locking differential. Equipment includes a swan-neck rear wing, Porsche Active Suspension Management (PASM), a front-axle lift system, Porsche Ceramic Composite Brakes, rear-axle steering, High Gloss California Gold-finished 20″ and 21″ GT3 center-lock alloy wheels, heated 18-way adaptive Sport Plus seats, a carbon-fiber roof, HD-Matrix Design LED headlights, the Chrono Package, a Bose sound system, and Porsche Communication Management with navigation. Previously listed on BaT in January 2026, this 992.2 GT3 has 19 miles and is offered again at no reserve in Arizona by the selling dealer, a Local Partner, with a window sticker copy, owner’s manuals, a clean Carfax report, and a clean Oregon title.

The car is finished in Oak Green Metallic Neo, and exterior details include a swan-neck rear wing, a carbon-fiber roof, body-color front airblades, carbon-fiber mirror caps and window triangles, HD-Matrix Design LED headlights, Exclusive Design taillights, and a rear diffuser with twin center-mounted exhaust outlets. Photos showing paint-meter readings are provided in the gallery below.

High Gloss California Gold-finished 20″ and 21″ GT3 center-lock alloy wheels are mounted with Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires. The car is equipped with rear-axle steering, a front-axle lift system, and Porsche Active Suspension Management (PASM). Braking is handled by yellow-finished Porsche-script calipers over cross-drilled and ventilated carbon-ceramic rotors.

The heated 18-way adaptive Sport Plus seats are trimmed in black and gray upholstery with “GT3” embroidery on the headrests, Racing Yellow seatbelts, and contrasting stitching that extends to the dashboard, center console, and door panels. Body-color trim accents the cabin, and additional appointments include automatic climate control, a Bose sound system, and Porsche Communication Management with navigation along with “Born in Flacht” puddle light projectors, ambient lighting, cruise control, aluminum pedals, carbon-fiber floor mats, and personalized carbon-fiber illuminated door sill guards.

The leather-wrapped GT Sport steering wheel features a drive-mode dial, and it fronts a configurable digital gauge cluster. A Sport Chrono stopwatch is mounted atop the center of the dash. The digital odometer indicates 19 miles.

The 4.0-liter flat-six features a 9k-rpm redline and was factory rated at 502 horsepower and 331 lb-ft of torque.

Power is routed to the rear wheels through six-speed manual transaxle and a Porsche Torque Vectoring Plus electronically locking differential.

The window sticker copy lists factory colors, options, and a total price of $295,990.

The Carfax report is free of accidents or other reported damage.

auctions.title: 22k-Mile 1996 Porsche 911 Turbo
auctions.thesis: This 1996 Porsche 911 Turbo was initially delivered to Tom Wood Porsche of Indianapolis, Indiana, and it was purchased by the current owner in 2018. The car shows 22k miles and is powered by a twin-turbocharged 3.6L flat-six that drives all four wheels through a six-speed manual transaxle with a lim...
auctions.history:
This 1996 Porsche 911 Turbo was initially delivered to Tom Wood Porsche of Indianapolis, Indiana, and it was purchased by the current owner in 2018. The car shows 22k miles and is powered by a twin-turbocharged 3.6L flat-six that drives all four wheels through a six-speed manual transaxle with a limited-slip differential. Finished in Arena Red Metallic over Classic Grey supple leather, the car is equipped with a fixed rear spoiler, a power sunroof, 18″ Technology hollow-spoke wheels, KW Variant 3 coilovers, heated power-adjustable front seats, automatic climate control, cruise control, and a Porsche Classic stereo. This 993 Turbo is now offered at no reserve on dealer consignment by a Local Partner, with a window sticker, a Porsche Certificate of Authenticity, the owner’s manuals, service records, the removed factory stereo, a clean Carfax report, and an Arizona title.

The 993 Turbo featured wider bodywork, revised bumper covers, and a fixed rear spoiler to accommodate top-mount intercoolers when compared with the standard 993 coupe. This example is finished in Arena Red Metallic (84S) and features integrated fog lights, a power sunroof, a rear window wiper, and polished exhaust outlets. The front lip was replaced in March 2015, and full-body paint-protection film was applied in November 2021.

The 18″ Technology hollow-spoke wheels feature wheel spacers and are wrapped with Continental ExtremeContact Sport tires showing 2021 date codes. The car features an Automatic Brake Differential (ABD), and the 993 was the first 911 to feature multi-link rear suspension. KW Variant 3 coilovers have also been installed. Braking is provided by Porsche-branded four-piston calipers over cross-drilled rotors.

The seats are trimmed in Classic Gray supple leather, which extends to the door panels, dashboard, and center console. Amenities include heated power-adjustable front seats, cruise control, and automatic climate control. A Porsche Classic stereo has been retrofitted, and the factory head unit is included in the sale. The trunk struts were replaced in April 2015.

The leather-wrapped steering wheel frames a central tachometer, a 200-mph speedometer, an analog Quarz clock, and gauges for oil pressure, oil temperature, oil level, and fuel level. The six-digit odometer shows 22k miles, approximately 10k of which were added under current ownership.

The twin-turbocharged 3.6L flat-six was factory equipped with Bosch Motronic fuel injection and was rated at 400 horsepower and 400 lb-ft of torque when new.

Power is sent to all four wheels through a six-speed manual transaxle with a limited-slip differential. The clutch slave cylinder was replaced in January 2026.

The window sticker lists initial delivery to Tom Wood Porsche of Indianapolis, Indiana, as well as factory colors, options, and a total of $110,891.

The Carfax report shows no accidents or damage.
There is currently a lien out on the title to the car, and the seller’s lender will need to be repaid before the title can be transferred to a new owner.
Filed under: 993, 993 turbo

The truck was repainted blue and kept in a collection for approximately three decades before being purchased ...
auctions.history: This 1966 Kenworth W925 is a Class 8, double-drive, long-haul tractor of traditional long-nose design that is said to have initially been used by Provo Steel & Supply Co. of Provo, Utah. The truck was repainted blue and kept in a collection for approximately three decades before being purchased by the seller in May 2025.
